name: Royal Free NHS–DeepMind Streams Deployment
description: Clinical deployment of the Streams application within Royal Free London NHS workflows for acute kidney injury alerting and response.

evidence_manifest.deployment_name: Streams deployment at Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust

sources.title: Royal Free NHS Foundation Trust enforcement action
Statement on agreement between Royal Free London and DeepMind
sources.authors: Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust
sources.publisher: Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust

sources.title: Streams app rolling out across the UK

transparency_explainability.claim: Public statements disclose deployment purpose and operating context, but technical detail remains limited.

fairness_nondiscrimination.claim: Deployment is clinically targeted and not explicitly framed around demographic fairness outcomes.

safety_robustness.claim: Deployment objective focuses on timely clinical alerts and operational reliability in care workflows.

privacy_data_governance.claim: Regulatory enforcement identified significant data-governance shortcomings in the deployment period.

human_agency_oversight.claim: Clinical staff remained responsible for diagnosis and treatment decisions.

accountability.claim: Formal regulatory action and remediation obligations establish explicit accountability pathways.

assumptions: Scores convert documented deployment and regulatory evidence into MEDF's Likert representation.
Evidence is interpreted for governance evaluation rather than clinical efficacy benchmarking.
The case is treated as a real hospital deployment with public, reproducible source provenance.
